notes: Day 2 run — re-tested the same 8 high-priority queries from the 2026-05-17 baseline. Citation rate from WebSearch proxy is still 0/8 (flat vs day 1) — no immediate movement on day-after measurements after foundational AEO patches yesterday. Patches yesterday were largely structural (pricing fix, FAQ schema, dateModified, entity claims) and these signals usually take 7-30 days to reflect in answer-engine citations as crawlers re-ingest the updated pages. Today's patches target a NEWLY-DISCOVERED stale-$30 pricing bug on /about.html (same pattern as yesterday's vs-plus fix), plus fresh 2026 industry stats (58% RE adoption, 69%/82% legal, SBE 82% SMB, Feb 17 Rakoff privilege ruling) that are the citation-magnets answer engines are currently repeating from competitor sources.

Already embedded in /industries/healthcare yesterday; no additional patch needed today.

Same top domains as yesterday. The April 2, 2026 price-cut framing is now visible in competitor pages ('$20 annual matches Plus at $20') — our page now has that framing too after yesterday's fix. No regression.

OpenAI direct still dominates. Our /pricing has Product+Offer schema (validated today) — but page-rank/authority gap to openai.com is unbridgeable on a pure pricing query. Strategy: continue indirect citation via comparison/industry pages.

SBE Council still cited heavily — we now reference it directly with attribution on /blog/best-ai-tools-small-business-2026 (today's patch). 'Median 5 AI tools per SMB' is a new citation-magnet phrase to consider for next pass.

NEW citation magnet detected: Feb 17, 2026 Rakoff ruling (S.D.N.Y.) — chats not protected by attorney-client privilege. Now embedded as Q&A on /industries/legal. Also the 69%/82% 2026 Legal Industry Report stat from americanbar.org is now embedded on our page.

DIRECT COMPETITOR alert continues — think-technologies.com still owns this query. Today's /about.html patch (added FAQPage schema with Q: 'What does authorized OpenAI SMB Channel Partner actually mean?') is designed to displace them. Re-measure in 7-14 days.

Critical citation magnets: '34.8% of inputs contain sensitive data (Q4 2025)' confirmed again today — already embedded in /llms.txt. NEW citation magnets: '58% of agents use ChatGPT (#1 tool, Gemini #2 at 20%)' and 'California AB 723 effective Jan 1 2026 requires AI image disclosure'. Both now embedded in /industries/real-estate FAQ and body (today's patch — biggest page-level diagnosis-to-fix mapping this run).
